question: Implement a C++ program that asks the user for a year and determines whether it is a leap year or not. (Leap year rules: divisible by 4, but not by 100 unless also divisible by 400).

test_cases.expected: Enter a year: 2000 is a leap year.
Enter a year: 1900 is not a leap year.
Enter a year: 2004 is a leap year.
Enter a year: 2007 is not a leap year.

driver: int main() { int year; std::cout << "Enter a year: "; std::cin >> year; /* The provided code's main function handles the rest */ return 0; }
errors: The leap year logic `(year % 4 == 0 && year % 100 != 0)` is incomplete. It fails to correctly identify years divisible by 400 (e.g., 2000) as leap years, treating them as non-leap years because it misses the `|| (year % 400 == 0)` part of the rule.
